title: Veil
tagline: A second-skin nude. The hand, unbothered.
story: Veil was born from the moment after — after the wedding, the meeting, the long day of being seen. The shade you wear when you no longer need to perform. A whisper of warmth that lets your hand speak first, and your voice second.
pair_with: Linen sheets, slow espresso, an unread book
editorial_review: “I bought it for a wedding and ended up wearing it for three weeks. It became part of how I wanted to dress.”

title: Whisper
story: Some women command attention by raising their voice. Others, by lowering it. Whisper is the shade for the latter — a barely-there blush that earns the second glance, not the first.
pair_with: Late lunches, listening more than speaking, the right wine
editorial_review: “It looks like nothing in the bottle and like everything once it dries.”

title: Linen
tagline: Warm cream. Made for slow mornings.
story: Linen was the first shade the team refused to share. The color of the sheets you don’t want to leave, the latte foam you ordered to-stay. A neutral that knows it is anything but ordinary — the quiet luxury everyone tries to copy but few can find.
pair_with: Cashmere socks, a paperback, a window seat
editorial_review: “I wear Linen the way some women wear their grandmother’s ring.”

title: Bare
tagline: Soft taupe with the weight of restraint.
story: Bare arrives at the dinner without trying to be the conversation. A taupe with a whisper of rose underneath — the color of a cashmere coat thrown over a black dress. Quiet, expensive, unforgettable in retrospect.
pair_with: A black dress, low candles, the second martini
editorial_review: “The color of someone who has decided what she wants and isn’t going to explain it.”

title: Petal
tagline: Dusty rose. The blush before the word.
story: Petal is the color of the second your face changes — when you’ve heard something you didn’t expect and your skin tells the truth before your mouth catches up. A blush translated into a shade.
pair_with: A letter you weren’t expecting, an honest answer, summer
editorial_review: “It does what blush does for the face. Petal does it for the hand.”

title: Sand
story: Sand was made for the seventh day of a trip you didn’t want to end. The color of skin touched by salt and afternoon light, the kind of warm that lives in your hand for weeks after you fly home.
pair_with: Coastal towns, linen shirts, the slow drink that follows
editorial_review: “I took it on holiday and brought back a bottle’s worth of evidence.”

title: Reign
tagline: The blood red that rewrote the rules.
story: Reign is the shade for the woman who has already decided. The red of the lipstick mark left on the rim of a glass, the seal at the bottom of a letter that means it. A color you don’t ask permission to wear.
pair_with: A tailored coat, an unread room, the last word
editorial_review: “Reign is the shade I put on when I need to stop second-guessing.”

title: Vampire
story: Vampire is what happens when a great red ages another decade. A wine so deep it reads as memory — the bottle opened for the conversation that changed everything. Worn by the women who don’t reschedule.
pair_with: A heavier perfume, a slow restaurant, the second bottle
editorial_review: “The darkest red I’ve owned that still looked like a red.”

title: Midnight
story: Midnight isn’t dark. It’s the color of dusk caught between hours — a shade so deep it reads black until the light hits, and then it confesses its wine underneath. A secret kept in plain sight.
pair_with: A leather jacket, the cab home, an honest 1 a.m.
editorial_review: “It looks like one color in the bottle and three on the hand.”

title: Velvet
story: Velvet is the shade you wear when the conversation matters. A burgundy with the depth of an old garnet, the kind of color that makes your hand the first thing remembered about the room.
pair_with: A long table, a strong opinion, an even temper
editorial_review: “I wore Velvet to a negotiation. It read for me.”

title: Obsession
tagline: Black cherry. The shade you keep returning to.
story: Obsession was tested seven times before we let it leave the lab. Not because it didn’t work — because it was too quiet. A black cherry that looks, at every angle, like something you’d want to keep. Then keep wearing.
pair_with: A repeated playlist, a familiar perfume, a habit you keep
editorial_review: “I bought one, ran out, bought three.”
